frameworks.name: Multitask Preplay
frameworks.one_line: counterfactual replay of unpursued accessible tasks to learn predictive representations

frameworks.name: predictive representation learning
frameworks.one_line: successor-style predictive features extended via counterfactual preplay over alternative tasks

frameworks.name: model-based planning
frameworks.one_line: traditional planning baseline that does not preemptively simulate unpursued tasks

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: agent learning framework within which preplay augments experience replay

core_question: Do humans (and artificial agents) preemptively learn solutions to accessible-but-unpursued tasks by counterfactually replaying experience as if pursuing them?
key_assumptions: Humans pursue few tasks at a time but encode information useful for accessible alternatives
Experience on one task can seed counterfactual simulation of another
Predictive representations support fast adaptive performance on later tasks
Task co-occurrence structure is shared across worlds and transferable
manipulations: Task pursued vs accessible-but-unpursued in a small grid-world
Whether participants knew they would later need to generalize
Environment complexity: grid-world vs partially observable 2D Craftax
Algorithm class: multitask preplay vs traditional planning vs predictive-representation baselines
key_findings: Multitask preplay better predicts human generalization to accessible-but-unpursued tasks than planning and predictive-representation baselines
Predictive advantage holds even when participants were unaware they would need to generalize
Findings replicate from grid-world to the partially observable 2D Craftax environment
Artificial agents using multitask preplay transfer behavior to novel Craftax worlds sharing task co-occurrence structure
interpretations: Humans leverage current experience to preemptively solve potential future tasks via counterfactual replay
Preplay yields predictive representations that enable rapid adaptation when task demands shift
